task_context.description: This is a comparative detector design and performance evaluation task for high-energy physics instrumentation. The user wants to design and simulate three different homogeneous electromagnetic calorimeters using different crystal materials (PbWO4, BGO, and CsI) to determine which would be optimal for precision electron and photon measurements at a future electron-positron collider. The task involves Monte Carlo simulation using Geant4 to characterize detector performance across multiple metrics including energy resolution, shower containment, and spatial resolution. This is specifically focused on homogeneous calorimeter designs where the crystal material serves as both absorber and active detector medium, unlike sampling calorimeters that alternate between absorber and detector layers.
task_context.goal: Determine the best crystal material choice for an electromagnetic calorimeter at the Future Circular Collider electron-positron (FCC-ee) experiment by comparing energy resolution, shower containment, and spatial characteristics of PbWO4, BGO, and CsI crystals
constraints: Use homogeneous calorimeter design only (no absorber layers)
Limit to 3 configurations maximum (one per material)
Energy sweep limited to 1, 5, and 20 GeV for tractable simulation
Use electrons (e-) as test particles in Geant4
Measure total detector depth for >99% energy containment
Choose from three geometry options: box, homogeneous_slab, or projective_tower topology

observations.content: This workflow systematically compares three homogeneous calorimeter materials through geometry generation, Geant4 simulation with energy sweeps, comprehensive analysis of energy resolution and shower characteristics, and final recommendation based on FCC-ee requirements. The projective tower geometry is chosen for optimal hermeticity and reconstruction performance in a collider environment.
Step completed successfully: Generate GDML geometry for PbWO4 homogeneous calorimeter using projective_tower topology for optimal shower containment

Step completed successfully: Run Geant4 simulation of electrons at 1, 5, and 20 GeV in CsI calorimeter
Step completed successfully: Analyze PbWO4 simulation data to extract energy resolution, shower containment, and Molière radius
Step completed successfully: Analyze BGO simulation data to extract energy resolution, shower containment, and Molière radius
Step completed successfully: Analyze CsI simulation data to extract energy resolution, shower containment, and Molière radius
Step completed successfully: Compare performance metrics across all three materials and determine optimal choice for FCC-ee
